Football.title: Chelsea have ‘unthinkable’ transfer fears put to rest as star signs new deal
Football.content:
Reece James has signed a new contract with Chelsea to put any fears of a summer exit to rest.

The Blues captain, who joined the club as a six-year-old, was previously out of contract in 2028.

2 James has made 35 appearances for Chelsea this season Credit: Getty

Chelsea have confirmed the 26-year-old has signed a new six-year deal which runs until 2032.



James said: "I am over the moon to have extended my contract - Chelsea means so much to me.

"I have always said I want my peak years to be here, and I truly believe we have everything it takes to build on our previous successes.

‘I’m excited for the future under this ownership, Sporting Directors, Coach and all the staff, and hopefully we will lift many more trophies together over the coming years."

Sporting directors Paul Winstanley and Laurence Stewart said: "On behalf of ownership and everyone connected to Chelsea, it is a proud moment for all of us to have Reece extend his contract.



"He is the leader of this team, both on and off the pitch. Reece knows what it means to wear this badge and, having committed his long-term future to Chelsea, we’re looking forward to continuing the journey and working towards further success together."

talkSPORT understands the Blues hierarchy expect a number of other contract extensions to follow - with Marc Cucurella and Levi Colwill thought to be top of their priority list.



James has been a star player for the Blues for many years after breaking through the academy.

The Englishman has started 19 Premier League games this season, registering two goals and four assists, appearing to put years of injury trouble behind him.



James has been regularly available for the first time since the 2021/22 campaign, playing a starring role across midfield and defence.

It is understood James' new deal aligns him with Chelsea's current incentivised wage structure.

He had been the only player at the club still on terms from the club's previous ownership.

Speaking on his journey with the club, last year he said: "It has been up and down along the way and it was tough to get into the first team and then stay there.

"But the journey has been an eventful and joyful one and I am so grateful to be in the position I am in now.



"It means everything to me, it's an amazing feeling – not only for me but also for my family."

The defender has been with the west London club for 19 years and the prospect of losing him in the summer started to feel real as talks over a new deal rumbled on.

Fear not, Chelsea fans

James is a big fan favourite among the Chelsea faithful with many not able to picture the team without him.

However, the Blues hierarchy would have been desperate to avoid letting James enter the final two years of his contract, and may have been forced to consider a sale to prevent him leaving for free in 2028.

The situation would have not have been unheard of after James' England teammate, Trent-Alexander Arnold, left boyhood club Liverpool for Real Madrid last summer at the end of his contract.



Ex-Chelsea defender Jason Cundy told fans prior to the contract extension that the new owners, BlueCo, would not allow that situation to manifest itself under the club's current financial model.

The co-host on our Sports Bar told Inside Chelsea: "Watch this space. He will be one of the highest-paid players at the club. They need to do something about that.

"We need to keep Reece James. We need to somehow find a way to get him to sign a new contract.

2 The Chelsea captain has won the Champions League, Conference League, FA Cups and most recently the Club World Cup Credit: Getty

"Look at what Trent did at Liverpool. If James gets into the last year of his contract, you can’t allow that. He could leave for nothing.



“Marc Guehi is worth three times what Man City just paid for him."

“One thing I understand from the new owners when they came in... is that they were astonished we let Antonio Rudiger and Andreas Christensen - worth more than £100m between them - walk on a free. They could not get their heads around that.

“I will be stunned and amazed if they allow Reece to have so much power without signing a new contract.

“It’s a fear. It’s a big concern. A football career is not long. Players want to know where they’re going.

“If Reece decides this project is not for him now, unfortunately, the club may well have to sell him in the summer. I don’t want to scaremonger or anything…"

Football.title: Disabled Aston Villa fan left Lille match in fear after seeing ‘blood all over the concourse’
Football.content:
Disabled Aston Villa fan left Lille match in fear after seeing ‘blood all over the concourse’

Gabby Agbonlahor has urged authorities to investigate the treatment handed out to travelling English football fans in UEFA competitions.

It comes after French police clashed with Aston Villa fans prior to Thursday’s Europa League clash with Lille - and one fan told us they left after ten minutes out of fear, seeing blood all over the concourse.

3 French police clash with Aston Villa fans before their match against Lille Credit: AFP

The Premier League side claimed a 1-0 victory in their round-of-16 first leg thanks to Ollie Watkins’ second-half winner.



However, the result was overshadowed by shocking footage from outside the Stade Pierre-Mauroy.

A video circulating on social media shows police using batons as they stop supporters from entering the stadium.

Tear gas also appears to be sprayed towards them before a gate is slammed shut in front of the waiting Villa fans.

Meanwhile, it has been claimed that tasers were also used on the travelling supporters.



talkSPORT has contacted Aston Villa for comment.

It is not the first time English fans have reported heavy-handed tactics while entering French stadia.

Liverpool supporters were penned in against perimeter fences and stuck in a motorway underpass prior to the 2022 Champions League final at the Stade de France.



And more recently Reds’ fans suffered further difficulties entering the Stade Velodrome for their Champions League clash at Marseille in January.

In November 2023, Newcastle fans were attacked in a bar by a masked group on the eve of their match at Paris Saint-Germain.



And Steve, who attended the game on Thursday night, phoned talkSPORT to give Breakfast co-host Agbonlahor their eye-witness account.

Disabled fan leaves

He said: “What did we see? Villa fans of all ages and they said, oh we've just been tear gassed, we've just been tear gassed. So we're thinking, oh blimey, what are we going to walk into here?

“And then they kind of let us through the next phase and you go to the next part where there's another cage where all the food outlets are and literally there was loads of stewards being very, very heavy-handed.

“The police were in the corner by that part so I didn't see what was happening there but there were some Villa fans in a really bad way. There was a lot of blood all over the concourse.

“So I said to my mate Trevor, who's disabled, I said, what do you want to do?



"And he was like, I don't want to go in. We did for about 10 minutes and do you know what? The atmosphere, it just wasn't great.

3 Liverpool fans were poorly treated by police at the Stade de France before the 2022 Champions League final Credit: AFP

“So literally we, and there were a lot of people, again of all ages, just saying I'm done, I'm not going to bother.

“So literally we turned around and the kind of decision that you make then where you think, is it really worth it?

"And then we came out and we ended up watching the rest of the game in a bar. So yeah, it wasn't nice, it wasn't pleasant but unfortunately it's kind of what we come to expect whenever we go away."



Asked if the heavy-handed police approach had become a common feature when following Villa in Europe, Steve said: "Yeah, it is. Like Bruges, Feyenoord, Rotterdam, like literally they pen you in.

“And to be honest, honestly, I know it's easy to say but Villa fans are good as gold. They go, the crack is good, they have a good time.

“But literally it's almost as if you're kind of being wound up a little bit. You're kind of being intimidated and it's really, it's really not the one.”

3 Gabby Agbonlahor was appalled by he treatment of Aston Villa fans in Lille on Thursday evening

Unfair treatment

Former Aston Villa striker Agbonlahor voiced his annoyance at the treatment dished out to English fans on the continent and spoke of his own experience during his trip to PSG’s Parc des Princes last season.



“It doesn't surprise me,” Agbonlahor told talkSPORT. “I would have to say, I went to PSG away last season in the Champions League, and everyone was on edge before the game. There were some incidents in local bars. Villa fans were attacked.

“But the police did do a good job of sort of getting us all in and getting us all out safely. But I'm not surprised. And what annoys me is that we get a bad name, English fans, when they go abroad.

“But I'm sure the Villa fans, and I had friends that were over there, just want to get into the game. But the way they treat you, and it's not just Villa, a lot of other fan bases can say the same. They treat you terribly.

“But when we, as police over here, Fenerbahce, they trashed the City ground, Nottingham Forest Stadium, early on in the season.

“I bet they got goody bags on the way out. That's how nice we are over here to fans that cause carnage and rip seats out. The English fans don't do that abroad. But they get treated so badly.



“And there's got to be something done about it.”

The former Villa star called upon the authorities to look over the footage that has circulated online in recent hours as proof of the unfair treatment.

“Surely the footage that's been put out, that can be sent and complaints being made, because kids would have been there.

"I know people that take their kids to European away games. And young kids. Going through that experience is not nice.

“I was at the PSG game last season and I remember like, I was with my missus actually, it was her birthday and we were kept in for like probably an hour and a half and then like you say, different sections and you finally get out and it's like a cage and Villa fans have got their hands through the cage of like, let us out.

“Sort of like, you feel like a caged animal. And it's like, they don't get that treatment over here, do they?”

News.title: Shocking moment on-duty police officer ROMPS next to his patrol car as CCTV of brazen tryst goes viral
News.content:
THIS is the scandalous moment CCTV catches a cop mid-romp with a civilian whilst on duty.

The randy young officer arranged to meet with the woman near the Cañuelas Golf Club in Buenos Aires, Argentina and proceeded to get hot and heavy on a patch of grass beside his patrol car.





Little did they know, the golf course security cameras were filming every second.

Shocking footage of the saucy rendezvous went viral online and triggered an Internal Affairs investigation.

The 22-year-old cop was promptly dismissed over his inappropriate conduct.

A police spokesperson said: “He stopped at the location for about 10 minutes with a civilian woman he arranged to meet.

“Patrol cars are equipped with a device that alerts the authorities when they stop for over half an hour, that’s why there wasn’t an alert in this case.

“We only found out when the images went viral.”

He is the second police officer to be fired in recent months.



In December, another 22-year-old cop from Cañuelas was dismissed for firing a shotgun into the air to celebrate Christmas.

He posted videos of himself firing four shots whilst dressed in civilian clothes, sparking public outcry.

News.title: OnlyFans model left for dead after ‘Dubai Porta Potty party’ horror makes incredible comeback as she poses in glam pics
News.content:
THE OnlyFans model left for dead after a suspected “Dubai Porta Potty party” has made a remarkable comeback.

Last year, Maria Kovalchuk, 21, was feared dead after she was discovered unconscious on a roadside in Dubai, suffering from catastrophic injuries.





The 21-year-old Ukrainian had her hair cut off with a knife and was scalped, while also suffering a broken spine and leg.

At the time, there were fears she had been a victim of one of the notorious “Porta Potty” parties.

These shadowy gatherings involve women, often aspiring models or influencers, who are lured to various settings with the promise of huge payments and then forced to perform degrading and violent sexual acts involving bodily fluids.



Now, against the odds, Maria has returned to the limelight.

She recently revealed she has travelled to Paris to study makeup, attending courses that include masterclasses from Chanel and Bobbi Brown.

Her recovery has been long and gruelling.

Maria spent months in Norway, where she lives in exile from her war-torn homeland of Ukraine, undergoing intensive treatment and rehabilitation.



In total, she endured ten major operations and was initially confined to a wheelchair.

A year on, she is back on her feet.

“At first, I felt literally shattered, like a broken doll, and I could not imagine my future life at all,” Maria told her followers.

“I kept wondering how I would live with all these injuries, with his scar, disfigured… how I would somehow go on with my life.

“But over time, my perception of the world began to change.



“I started paying attention to my inner self and realised that what matters most is your inner state – what you learn and what you feel.

“Of course, there were difficulties with this, too.

“But I managed thanks to the support of my loved ones and my friends.”

Maria claims she was tortured by wealthy Russians during a three-day party in Dubai – contradicting earlier speculation that “sheikhs” had been responsible for her injuries.

The Russians have denied abusing her, and no one has ever been brought to justice over the injuries that almost killed her.



While she has almost no memory of the alleged torture, Maria said she missed her flight to Thailand while staying at the luxury Five Jumeirah Village hotel.

She then said a 19-year-old man offered her a place to stay, claiming his father could fly her out on a private jet.

However, Maria alleges that once she went with him, the man – along with several others – instead started torturing her.

CCTV footage that could have shown what happened was allegedly erased after three months, Maria claims, leaving no video evidence.

“The police waited until the cameras were automatically wiped… So now there’s no evidence,” she said.



According to her mother, Anna, her daughter underwent “three operations on one leg, three on the other, two on her spine, and one on her shoulder blade”.

The family has also revealed that Maria’s medical bills were fully covered by Dubai police – further fuelling speculation online about a possible cover-up.

Anna says she was threatened after speaking publicly about the case.

After her daughter went public, she received a disappearing message on her phone.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We’ll find you even in Norway,” it warned.



Dubai police claimed to have detained the accused young men – briefly – but released them the following day, with the case reportedly closed.

News.title: Horror moment hot air balloon CRASHES into building causing woman to be thrown from basket & fatally plunge onto rooftop
News.content:
THIS is the moment a hot air balloon crashes into a building as woman is thrown from the basket in a horror fatal plunge.

Champion hot air balloon pilot Jagoda Gancarek, 28, was killed in the disaster ride in Poland on Monday morning.





The white, green and yellow hot air balloon can be seen flying dangerously low over a block of flats.

The balloon crashes its way to street level.

Deflated, it is tangled in a tree and draped over a bus.

Jagoda and two other women were on board when the balloon reportedly hit an apartment block.

Upon impact, Jagoda spilled from the basket and fell onto the rooftop.

When paramedics arrived by helicopter, they were unable to save her despite frantic resuscitation efforts.

The balloon continued to crash land with the two other women still clinging to the basket.



They later miraculously stepped out without serious injury after making an emergency landing on the ground in the centre of Zielona Góra, western Poland.

The three women had been flying for an hour and a half from Lubuskie Region Aero Club when they got into trouble.

Investigations are now underway by Polish police and the country’s State Commission for Aircraft Accident Investigation.

Police spokesperson Małgorzata Stanisławska said: “As a result of the accident, a 28-year-old woman died after falling from the basket onto the roof of one of the buildings.



“Unfortunately, despite resuscitation efforts, she could not be saved.”

The basket lay on a street next to a bus, with the torn Czech-made balloon tangled in the branches of a tree above.

Jagoda was an instructor who spoke about the “silence and calm” on balloon flights.



“You’re left alone with your thoughts. It’s an incredible feeling,” she previously said.

“The adrenaline is there, of course, but above all, you just focus on getting things done.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Last year, she was crowned Polish Women’s Champion at the 11th Women’s Ballooning Championships in Nałęczów.

The club said in a statement: “We will remember Jagoda as a warm and always smiling person, full of passion and a love for flying.”

News.title: Husband ‘told wife he would cut up and dissolve her body in acid before she was driven to suicide by tsunami of abuse’
News.content:
TRAGIC DEATH Husband ‘told wife he would cut up and dissolve her body in acid before she was driven to suicide by tsunami of abuse’

A HUSBAND told his wife he would cut up her body and dissolve it acid before she took her own life after a “tsunami of abuse”, a court heard.

Christopher Trybus is on trial accused of manslaughter after Tarryn Baird was found hanged at home in Swindon, Wiltshire, at the age of 34.





The 43-year-old allegedly trapped her in an abusive relationship by isolating her from friends and family, controlling her finances and monitoring her whereabouts.

Trybus is also accused of twice raping Tarryn and threatening her with violence in a twisted bid to make her stay.

Giving evidence, Tarryn’s mum Michelle Baird told Winchester Crown Court how she had seen bruises on her daughter’s body and said Trybus just saw her as a “trophy”.



She said that a “couple of weeks” before her daughter’s death, Tarryn told her Trybus had threatened to kill her and dispose of her body.

The mum added: “Tarryn said Christopher said he would ‘snap my neck in a heartbeat, cut up my body, dissolve it in acid and nobody would find me’.

“I said to Christopher ‘Are you sick? If I don’t find my daughter I will come looking for her’.

“He laughed and began to tell me how he would do it. He wasn’t saying it in a jokey way at all.”



Michelle sobbed as she told that jurors that when she discussed arrangements for Christmas, Tarryn said to her: “There won’t be a Christmas this year.”

Following a suicide attempt in September 2017, Michelle said they had a family meeting because she was “very concerned” for her.

The mum told the court she suggested Tarryn needed 24-hour care and wanted to look into going to The Priory as they could afford it.

But Michelle added: “[Tarryn] told me Christopher thought it was too expensive, £6,000 a week, and he thought it wasn’t the right fit for her.”

The mum also described how her daughter became “extremely concerned” when a police officer “wanted to arrest” Trybus in September 2017 after her daughter’s counsellor reported she had been abused.



Michelle said following Tarryn’s death, Trybus stayed in her home for six months and took his wedding ring off after just two weeks.

Jurors heard previously how Trybus carried out a “tsunami” of abuse against his wife, including “extensive and escalating controlling, coercive and manipulative behaviour including sexual violence of two rapes and other sexual assaults”.

The couple, both from South Africa, moved to the UK in 2007 and married in 2009.

Trybus allegedly controlled “many aspects of their relationship” using the “threat and fear of physical and sexual violence”.

This led to a deterioration in her “already weakened mental state” and was a “cause of her deciding that she should take her own life”, it was said.



The court heard that in November 2017, Tarryn was found dead alongside a heartbreaking note.

It read: “To my family, I am so sorry but I just couldn’t take it any more.

“I know you may not understand this but I just can’t explain the dark cloud that is over me.

“Please don’t let this break you but know I am now free. Nothing any of you could have done could have changed this, please just know that.

“I love you and please forgive me.”



Tom Little KC, prosecuting, said Tarryn had “not managed to escape” her husband – “despite seriously considering doing so on a number of occasions”.

How to get help EVERY 90 minutes in the UK a life is lost to suicide It doesn’t discriminate, touching the lives of people in every corner of society – from the homeless and unemployed to builders and doctors, reality stars and footballers. It’s the biggest killer of people under the age of 35, more deadly than cancer and car crashes. And men are three times more likely to take their own life than women. Yet it’s rarely spoken of, a taboo that threatens to continue its deadly rampage unless we all stop and take notice, now. If you, or anyone you know, needs help dealing with mental health problems, the following organisations provide support: CALM, www.thecalmzone.net, 0800 585 858

Heads Together,www.headstogether.org.uk

HUMEN www.wearehumen.org

Mind, www.mind.org.uk, 0300 123 3393

Papyrus, www.papyrus-uk.org, 0800 068 41 41

Samaritans,www.samaritans.org, 116 123

He added: “[The abuse] took place over a sustained period of time behind closed doors and all of it during the course of a marriage.

“It led ultimately in November 2017 to a woman in just her thirties and whose name is Tarryn Baird taking her own life by hanging.

“She was the defendant’s wife and the prosecution say that the defendant is legally responsible for her death.”

But Trybus has blamed Tarryn’s injuries on “rough sex” and claims it was all consensual.



He also believes his wife presented a “facade” to health professionals because of her own mental health issues, the court heard.

Trybus denies manslaughter, controlling and coercive behaviour and two charges of rape.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The trial continues.

If you are affected by any issues in this article, please contact the Samaritans on 116 123 or the Freephone National Domestic Abuse Helpline, run by Refuge on 0808 2000 247 any time, day or night

News.title: Keir Starmer DIDN’T interview Mandelson before handing him ambassador job despite being warned about Epstein link
News.content:
SIR Keir Starmer didn’t personally interview Lord Mandelson for his top US ambassador job despite warnings over his links to disgraced Jeffrey Epstein.

The ex-Labour peer didn’t undergo a rigorous test with the Prime Minister before he was given his job in Washington DC.



The PM has been under intense pressure over the appointment with the Tories demanding a sleaze probe into whether he misled MPs.



A host of red flags over Mandelson taking the job were raised in a “due diligence” report but no discussion with the PM took place.

It showed that Mandelson’s relationship with the financier carried on after his conviction and he even stayed at his property.

Number 10 today said: “The full process at the time of the appointment was followed. There was no requirement for a formal interview with the Prime Minister.”

It comes after The Times reported that Starmer gave close aides Morgan McSweeney and Matthew Doyle the task of vetting links to sex offender Epstein.

Files that emerged this week revealed National Security Adviser Jonathan Powell believed the appointment process was “weirdly rushed”.

It comes as the Tories accused the government of a “cover up” over the release this week of files relating to Mandelson taking on the role.

Shadow Cabinet Minister Alex Burghart has written to the sleaze watchdog Sir Laurie Magnus demanding that there was “missing” correspondence.

There was also an absence of comments from the PM relating to Mandelson but Downing Street said this didn’t mean “advice was not read or considered”.



The Prime Minister’s official spokesman reiterated today that Sir Keir and his team respond to advice in a “range of different ways”.

He said: “The absence of comments in a particular box doesn’t mean the advice wasn’t read or considered.

“There’s a range of different ways in which the Prime Minister and his senior team responds to advice throughout the day.”

The spokesman would not be drawn on whether private emails or WhatsApp messages would be part of future releases.

The PM has taken full responsibility for giving Mandelson the job.



Lord Mandelson was arrested last month on suspicion of misconduct in public office, having been accused of passing sensitive information on to Epstein during his time as business secretary under Gordon Brown’s premiership.

He was subsequently bailed, but later released from his bail conditions, although he remains under investigation.

News.title: Boy, 15, who ‘stabbed 15-year-old girl in back with large carving knife in school toilet area’ appears in court
News.content:
SCHOOL HORROR Boy, 15, who ‘stabbed 15-year-old girl in back with large carving knife in school toilet area’ appears in court

A TEEN boy has appeared in court charged with stabbing a 15-year-old girl in the back with a carving knife in a school toilet area.

The 15-year-old, who can’t be named, was arrested on Wednesday after armed police swooped on Thorpe St Andrew School in Norwich.





He appeared at Norwich Magistrates’ Court today charged with attempted murder and possession of a knife on school premises.

Prosecutor Josephine Jones said CCTV of a toilet area at the school showed a boy “all in black wearing a black mask” carrying a “carving knife with a lengthy blade”.

She added: “The victim had been stabbed to the back.”

Ms Jones said the defendant fled but was arrested by police nearby as the school was placed in lockdown.

A search at his home allegedly showed information that he was “looking for specific victims”, the court was told.

The teen, who spoke only to confirm his name, address and date of birth, was remanded into youth detention accommodation.

He will next appear at Norwich Crown Court on April 10.

The horror unfolded at around 10.20am on Wednesday after the girl was stabbed.



Teachers barricaded classroom doors and some students hid under desks as the school was plunged into lockdown.

The girl was taken to hospital with minor injuries and has since been discharged.

Norfolk Police have said they are continuing to conduct reassurance patrols both at the school and in the area over the coming days.

Thorpe St Andrew is an 11-18 school, plus sixth form, around three miles east of Norwich.

The mixed secondary school received a “Good” rating in its last Ofsted report.

News.title: M5 closed outside major UK airport as air ambulance lands after ‘11-vehicle’ crash with drivers warned of long delays
News.content:
THE M5 has been closed near a major UK airport as an air ambulance lands following a “11 vehicle” crash.

At least two people have been injured, according to the police.



Drivers have been warned to expect long delays.



It happened on the southbound carriageway between junction 28, for the A373 Honiton Road at Cullompton, and junction 29, for the A30 at Exeter, at around 11.30am.

Ambulance and fire crews are also at the scene, near to Exeter airport.

One witness has said the crash involved “multiple vehicles” and looked “very serious”.

Another witness said: “Crash between Cullompton and Exeter.”



One driver claimed they’ve been stuck in traffic on the southbound carriageway for more than 90 minutes.

Graham Norman, a retired firefighter from Braunton, Devon, told the BBC: “We’ve been stationary since about midday.”

He added: “We’re in the middle of it, we could see the rear of the ambulances in the distance but we think they’ve kind of moved on to hospital now.

“We did hear what we thought was the air ambulance landing, although we didn’t see it, but we counted about five ambulances, I think three fire engines and several police cars.”



Devon and Somerset Fire and Rescue Service said it had sent three appliances.

A spokesperson said: “One person was trapped and cut free of their vehicle by crews using hydraulic cutting equipment, they have been transported to hospital by air ambulance.”

National Highways said the southbound carriageway has been closed to allow emergency services to work safely at the scene.

It has not given an estimate for when the road will be reopened.

Motorists are urged to find alternative routes and to avoid the area.



It said on its website drivers should exit the M5 southbound at J28 and turn right at the traffic light controlled junction to join the A373 and follow diversion signs.

Inrix said in an update: “M5 Southbound partially blocked, severe delays due to accident from J28 A373 Honiton Road (Cullompton/Service Area) to J29 A30 Honiton Road (Exeter/Honiton).

“Congestion to J27 (Tiverton).

“Surrounding routes around Cullompton as traffic seeks an alternative.

“Sensors show that traffic can pass the scene after a full closure earlier, despite reports that the motorway is fully closed.



“Prior to 12:00 traffic was held and later confirmed fully closed until prior to 13:50.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Restrictions Northbound have been lifted.”

In an update from National Highways is said that all lanes northbound had been reopened.

News.title: Andrew, Mandelson & Epstein pictured lounging in bathrobes in bombshell first known photograph of three men together
News.content:
A NEW picture appears to show Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Peter Mandelson sitting in bathrobes alongside Jeffrey Epstein.

It is believed to be the first known image of the three men together and was found in the Epstein files released earlier this year.





The three men can be seen sitting around a wooden table on some decking outside.

It is understood to have been taken between 1999 and 2000 before Epstein was imprisoned for soliciting prostitution from a minor.

The photo is believed to have been taken at Martha’s Vineyard, reports ITV.

The picture was included in the dossier of thousands of files on Jeffrey Epstein released by the US Department of Justice.



Mandelson and Andrew appear to be seen wearing white bathrobes while Epstein is dressed in a shirt and jumper.

The image bears a resemblance to another picture featuring Mandelson in a similar white robe sitting alongside Epstein.

The previous image, showing the disgraced Labour peer, was uncovered in a file labelled “Epstein birthday book.”

The extent of the relationship between Epstein and the two men was uncovered earlier this year when the US Department of Justice released thousands of documents relating to the paedophile financier.



Both Andrew and Mandelson have previously been pictured alongside the paedophile financier separately but the three have never previously been seen together in photographs.

Following the document dump, Lord Mandelson was sacked from his role as US Ambassador.

Meanwhile, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was stripped of his royal titles and removed from his stately country pad.

Both Peter Mandelson and the former prince have since been arrested over allegations of misconduct in a public office.

Both men have strongly and consistently denied any wrongdoing.



Andrew was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in a public office on his 66th birthday in February.

The shamed ex-prince was quizzed in custody before he was released under investigation from the Aylsham police station later the same day.



Cops previously said they were reviewing claims Andrew allegedly shared sensitive information with his paedo pal Jeffrey Epstein while serving as the UK’s trade envoy.

Officers also confirmed at the time that the force was separately probing allegations a woman was trafficked to the UK by Epstein to have a sexual encounter with Andrew.

After the bombshell arrest officers went on to raid other royal residences in Windsor and Norfolk.

Essex Police, the Metropolitan Police, Surrey, Thames Valley, Norfolk and Bedfordshire Police are all understood to have been assessing evidence within the Epstein files’ three million pages.

Their checks include up to 90 flights into Stansted’s private terminal by the jet owned by Andrew’s paedo pal.



Of those, 15 were after the US financier was jailed in 2008 for soliciting underage sex.

Cops also raided two properties linked to Lord Peter Mandelson earlier this year — one in London and another in Wiltshire.

Mandelson was later arrested on suspicion of misconduct in a public office.

The-72-year-old resigned from the Labour party and the House of Lords in disgrace in February after the release of the Epstein files.

He was sacked from his Washington role last September and now faces a police probe as the snowballing scandal deepens.



Mandelson has since been released from custody and his team has confirmed his passport has been returned.

Documents in the Epstein files appeared to show the former ambassador passing confidential information to Epstein while he was Business Secretary.

Hundreds of files relating to Mandelson and his time as an Ambassador have now been dropped after pressure was piled on the Prime Minister to justify his appointment to the role.

The Mandelson files release has piled new pressure on Keir Starmer amid accusations of a “cover up.”

Now, the Prime Minister faces mounting pressure as his decision to appoint Mandelson is scrutinised with documents revealing concerns were raised about the former Labour peer’s relationship with Epstein.



A host of red flags over Mandelson taking the job were raised in a “due diligence” report but no discussion with the PM took place.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Meanwhile, opposition leader Kemi Badenoch has blasted the PM, calling the release of the documents a “cover up.”

News.title: American living in the UK stunned as he receives 'highest honour possible'
News.content:
American living in the UK stunned as he receives 'highest honour possible'

"Something amazing has happened as an American in the UK... Something that has taken years of hard work, trying to fit in, working on understanding of such a wonderful, beautiful culture. I've been given the highest honour the British have to give."

View Image People loved the heartwarming story shared by the American man living in the UK (stock image) (Image: Getty Images)

Uprooting your life and moving to an entirely different country can be daunting, and it's understandable to feel like an outsider in a new culture. But one American man in the UK has spoken of his pride at receiving what he described as "the highest honour possible" from his British in-laws.

‌



The man, who posts on social media as yankeedoodleintheuk, explained the heartwarming story on Instagram. In the video caption he wrote: "Something amazing has happened as an American in the UK... Something that has taken years of hard work, trying to fit in, working on understanding of such a wonderful, beautiful culture. I've been given the highest honour the British have to give."

‌



The clip also displayed the caption: "The British gave me the highest honour possible... and my life will never be the same." Opening the video, he said: "Something beautiful just happened to me the other day and I think it finally means I've been accepted into British society.

‌



"Nothing too dramatic, nothing too out of this world, just something simple. Something that made me feel decent.

"It was my mother-in-law's birthday party the other day, and we wanted to celebrate with the most British cake possible - the Colin the Caterpillar cake."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Marks and Spencer's legendary Colin the Caterpillar cake is a fixture of celebrations up and down the UK. Described by the retailer as "the original and best", M&S first started selling the cake in 1990. It's made up of large chocolate sponge roll with chocolate buttercream, covered in thick milk chocolate and chocolate beans - completed by the all-important white chocolate feet and face.

The man continued: "I couldn't go to the party because our nephews have chicken pox and I've never had them before. [I was] wishing I was there having some of the cake, so I asked my wife 'can you just bring me a slice back home?'

"So hours, hours go by and she returns home and says 'I've brought you back something, a surprise'. She opened up the tub and it wasn't just a slice or a slice of his belly.

‌



"It was his head! It was his lovely head!

"At that moment I was just like 'oh my God, Colin the Caterpillar's head!' It's the piece I've never had before, it's the relic, it's the Holy Grail, it's something beautiful.

"I took it in my hands and looked at it and like a little tear came down my cheek, and I thought 'this is my moment, my family fully loves me. I've got Colin the Caterpillar's head'."

Article continues below

People in the comments loved the story. One said: "They really love you." Another wrote: "You have arrived brotha!"

Someone else said: "You are the son she didn’t give birth to or adopted! You are loved, respected and valued!"

And another wrote: "Welcome, now just don't eat a Terry's chocolate orange like an apple and you're all good."

News.title: Double tragedy as teen finds girlfriend's body - and dies two weeks later
News.content:
Double tragedy as teen finds girlfriend's body - and dies two weeks later

Tom Dingle, 19, was found dead in woods a few miles from his home on Christmas Day, weeks after discovering girlfriend Natasha Braithwaite dead in her Lancaster University halls

View 3 Images Tom Dingle, 19, was found dead weeks after discovering his girlfriend's body (Image: LancsLive)

A teenager who was found dead on Christmas Day had discovered his girlfriend's body in her bedroom just weeks beforehand, an inquest has heard.

‌



Tom Dingle, from Appley Bridge in West Lancashire, was reported missing on December 13 last year. Lancashire Police launched a search operation following his disappearance before his body was discovered by a dog walker in woodland in the nearby village of Dalton on December 25.

‌



An inquest heard this week how the 19-year-old found his girlfriend dead on November 20, just weeks prior to his disappearance.

‌



Natasha Braithwaite, originally from Lincoln, had been a first-year undergraduate fine art student at Lancaster University, with the 19-year-old only weeks into her course when she was found in her room at Bowland College, Lancs Live reports.

Friends had described Natasha as a "sweet, artistic and talented girl".

View 3 Images Natasha Braithwaite was found dead in her halls of residence in November last year (Image: MEN Media)

‌



Family rally round after police sergeant mum-of-two found dead by husband READ MORE :

Premium Bonds account change warning over 'fast-approaching' deadline READ MORE :

During an inquest on March 10 into her death at Preston Crown Court, it emerged that the night before her death, Tom, who also studied at the university, had arranged to see her but fell asleep.

He awoke and tried to ring Natasha but when he couldn't reach her he went round to her halls of residence. He managed to rouse some of her flatmates and they gained access to her room, where they found her unresponsive in bed.

Paramedics rushed to the scene, but Natasha was pronounced dead upon arrival. The inquest was told she had been grappling with post-traumatic stress following an incident while in Abu Dhabi the previous December.

‌



Natasha had been taking drugs, and toxicology reports showed she had lethal levels of morphine in her body when she died.

View 3 Images Tom's family described him as an 'incredibly intelligent and caring young man' (Image: Lancashire Police)

Giving a summary prepared by a clinical scientist who analysed the findings, Assistant Coroner Sara Sutherland said: "The concentration of total morphine; that is morphine and its metabolites, is within the range encountered in fatalities attributed to the use of morphine. The compound taken in this case is likely to be heroin."

‌



In a family statement read by the coroner, Natasha was said to have appeared "excited" about starting university. She had been making future plans, including compiling her Christmas list, and had recently begun a relationship with Tom, with friend describing the pair as "inseparable".

Tom gave a statement to officers about Natasha weeks before he died, explaining how the couple had crossed paths at a fresher's night and characterised her as "a very kind girl who was extroverted".

Detective Constable Kirsten Bell said paraphernalia discovered in her room was thought to be connected to heroin use.

‌



The coroner delivered a verdict of a drug-related death and stated: "I have no doubt that Natasha was well-liked, extremely well-loved and would have been successful at uni. It is absolutely tragic."

In an online book of condolence set up by Lancaster University after Natasha's death, a flatmate said: "I truly don't think I've ever met a soul as beautiful, gentle, and caring as hers. She always greeted me with the warmest smile and I will forever cherish the little moments we shared."

A childhood mate penned: "Natasha was such a huge part of my early life. Her friendship and the bond we had truly shaped my childhood and who I am today. She is a true friend and a beautiful human-being, inside and out. The memories I have of her are a credit to her unique, bubbly and warm character."

‌



An inquest into Tom's death began in January, with a conclusive inquest set to take place at the same court on April 9.

In a written tribute, Tom's family said: "Tom was an incredibly intelligent and caring young man who had his whole life ahead of him. Tom's death has left a hole in our hearts which will never be filled. Christmas, and life as a whole, will never be the same without our Tom."

Bowland College, where Tom was studying at Lancaster University, also paid tribute to him on social media. A spokesperson said: "We are deeply saddened to share that a tragic update has been provided by Lancashire Constabulary in relation to the search for the missing Bowland College first year student Tom Dingle.

Article continues below

"His death will be a tremendous loss to friends and staff members who had the privilege to get to know him during his time at the University, and the Bowland community are with those affected by this sudden loss."

*If you're struggling and need to talk, the Samaritans operate a free helpline open 24/7 on 116 123. Alternatively, you can email jo@samaritans.org or visit their site to find your local branch.

News.title: Keir Starmer hits back as US eases Russian sanctions amid oil price spike
News.content:
Keir Starmer hits back as US eases Russian sanctions amid oil price spike

View 2 Images Keir Starmer's spokesman said allies must maintain the pressure on Russia (Image: Getty Images)

Downing Street has hit back at the US decision to loosen sanctions on Russian oil as Yvette Cooper accused the Kremlin and Iran of attempting to "hijack the global economy".

‌



Oil prices have rocketed to around $100 a barrel as energy markets were rocked by Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz - a crucial shipping lane. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent scrambled to curb the economic fallout by allowing temporary authorisation for countries to buy Russian oil already loaded on vessels at sea

‌



But Keir Starmer's spokesman said allies must maintain pressure on Russia and its war chest amid fears the Kremlin will exploit this opportunity to raise funds for its war on Ukraine. It marks another dividing line between Britain and the US after Mr Starmer refused to allow UK military bases to be used in initial strikes against Iran.

‌



View 2 Images Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper during a visit to a British military base on the edge of Riyadh in Saudi Arabia (Image: PA)

Asked if Mr Starmer was disappointed by the US move on sanctions, the PM's official spokesman said: "It's obviously a decision for the US, but our position is clear. All partners should maintain pressure on Russia and its war chest.

"Our support for Ukraine is degrading Russia's ability to wage war around the globe, both militarily and financially. The best way to continue to stop Russia supporting hostile actors is to continue on collective pressure and end the war in Ukraine."

‌



Asked to rule out the UK weakening its sanctions regime, the PM's spokesman said: "Our sanctions remain, there's no question about that. We remain committed to exerting that maximum economic pressure."

Earlier, Energy Minister Michael Shanks said: "This is an absolutely critical moment in the Russian aggression against Ukraine and sanctions are important.

"What we absolutely can't have is Putin sitting in the Kremlin thinking this is an opportunity to invest more in the war machine."

‌



The Foreign Secretary also issued a warning over the Kremlin's ties to Iran. Speaking on a visit to Saudi Arabia, Ms Cooper said: "We have seen these links between Russia and Iran over an extended period of time.

"We're seeing it in terms of technology, we see it in terms of the approach, we see it in terms of these kinds of tactics, and we see it in terms of the way these two states try to support each other and try to benefit together from attempting to hijack the global economy."

She added: "We are very clear about the threat from both Russia and from Iran to the global economy and to all of our wellbeing."

Article continues below

It comes as RAF jets widened their patrol of the skies to Bahrain on Thursday night to hunt Iranian drones.

Downing Street said: "Overnight, UK jets flew air defence sorties specifically in defence of Bahrain for the first time. This saw Typhoon jets patrolling the skies for Iranian drones heading towards Bahrain.

"British Typhoon and F35 jets are now flying in defence of British people, bases and our partners, moving in Qatar, Cyprus, UAE, Jordan and Bahrain."

News.title: King Charles hails groundbreaking sustainable innovations at global summit
News.content:
King Charles hails groundbreaking sustainable innovations at global summit

The King has been joined by more than 300 of the world's leading CEOs to discuss sustainable initiatives that will help fight climate change

View 4 Images King Charles founded the sustainable initiative five years ago (Image: AP)

King Charles attended the conclusion of the second Annual Sustainable Markets Initiative Exhibition (SMI) and Reception, at Hampton Court Palace, after a two-day summit with global industry and finance leaders and government representatives.

‌



The monarch marvelled at exhibits of innovations developed by industries, including sustainable textiles, carbon capture models and an electric aeroplane. Charles was so engrossed that the tour of the exhibit overran, as he was heard saying “fantastic” and “magnificent” several times. The King appeared wowed by a scale model of a machine that will clear up space debris, and marvelled at a replica of the Blue Origin space shuttle - which famously fired Katy Perry and Gayle King into orbit.

‌



View 4 Images King Charles attended the second Annual Sustainable Markets Initiative Exhibition (Image: PA)

‌



At the event, Charles was shown a model of the space debris removal satellite, called Elsa-M, which has been designed and built by Astroscale UK. Nick Shave, UK managing director of the company, said that the King had previously visited its site at Harwell Science and Innovation Campus in Oxfordshire.

Mr Shave said: “He remembered visiting us four years ago so I told him how much we developed on. He was pleased to see we’re making progress – he seemed very interested.”

The King later joined CEOs, 50 of whom were from China, to discuss what they have achieved during the two-day conference. He then met more guests in The Haunted Gallery and early shook hands with former US Secretary of State John Kerry.

‌



View 4 Images The monarch viewed a replica of Blue Origin's New Shepard, fully reusable suborbital rocket system (Image: PA)

The King launched the SMI to encourage private companies to invest in sustainability in 2020. The Sustainable Markets Initiative’s Roundtables and Exhibition was launched last year to showcase private investment firms and initiatives.

Sustainable Markets Initiative, CEO Jen Jordan-Saifi said: "We were absolutely delighted that His Majesty joined us today to see first-hand the remarkable support behind the Sustainable Markets Initiative and the exhibition showcasing the work of our member companies.

‌



"His Majesty founded the SMI with a clear vision - that the private sector must play a decisive role in delivering the sustainable transition.

View 4 Images Charles viewed models of ships, including the World's first ammonia fuelled shipping vessel (Image: PA)

Article continues below

"What we are seeing at this Summit is that vision in action, with CEOs from around the world coming together to deliver practical solutions and real progress”.

Former Secretary of State John Kerry has joined the Ocean Stewardship Initiative (OSI) as a Champion. Earlier, Charles also convened the Fashion Task Force at Buckingham Palace to discuss major progress across flagship projects advancing regenerative fashion and traceability through Digital Product Passports (DPP).

News.title: Shocking moment driver speeds at 100mph on motorway - then falls asleep
News.content:
Shocking moment driver speeds at 100mph on motorway - then falls asleep

Maldini Fernandes, 31, sped down the M4 at 100mph, with video footage capturing him flashing lights at drivers in front of him 20 minutes before falling asleep at the wheel

Dashcam video shows moment lorry driver fell asleep in the M4

Dascham footage has captured the shock moment a driver sped down a motorway at 100mph - before falling asleep at the wheel as other cars zoomed by.

‌



Maldini Fernandes, 31, has been convicted of dangerous driving after he travelled down the mddle of a live motorway, with the driver caught clocking up a speed of 100mph while blasting down the M4 in the early hours of January 18. Footage captured him flashing his lights at other motorists before coming to a halt at 1.20am.

‌



He stopped at Junction 9 for Maidenhead and Junction 11 for Reading and sat in an active lane while other vehicles soared past, with police saying he dropped off for up to 20 minutes.

‌



Diesel has jumped 16p in past fortnight but petrol only 7p - this is why READ MORE :

View 3 Images The driver sped up to around 100mph on the M4, before falling asleep (Image: Thames Valley Police / SWNS)

A spokesperson for Thames Valley Police (TVP) said that "in total, he was asleep for 20 minutes". Fernandes, from Southall, West London, was later convicted of driving a vehicle in a dangerous manner and sentenced to 30 weeks imprisonment, suspended for 12 months.

He also failed to provide breath sample in custody, and was also sentenced to 12 weeks imprisonment, suspended for 12 months, and has a requirement to pay over £200 in costs.

‌



Footage released following his sentencing shows the road blurring beneath the car as it reached high speeds earlier this year, with other vehicles in front of him moving out of his way as he flashed lights at them from behind. TVP said he was also disqualified from driving for three years and has to carry out 150 hours of unpaid work.

View 3 Images Police said Fernandes fell asleep at around 1am on January 18 (Image: Thames Valley Police / SWNS)

A force spokesperson added: "Thankfully, on this occasion no one was seriously injured or killed. If you're driving while tired, please take a break."

‌



Driving fatigue is a major factor in road incidents, more so than drink or drugs, according to Professor Steven Lockley, an expert in sleep and circadian rhythm research at the University of Surrey. In 2024, Professor Lockley warned that holidaymakers should be concsious of driving at night or continuing to drive while tired.

National Highways said that year that fatigue-related deaths surge in the summer months, between June and August, with 59 percent more than there are between December and February.

View 3 Images Police have called on people to 'please take a break' while driving at night (Image: Thames Valley Police / SWNS)

Article continues below

Sheena Hague, director of road safety at National Highways, said that fatigue can impact people on multiple levels, and can lead to "dangerous mistakes". She said: "Fatigue behind the wheel can be deadly. It’s not just about falling asleep, tiredness slows your reactions, clouds your judgement, and can lead to dangerous mistakes."

She called on drivers to take precautions on the road, adding: "That’s why we’re encouraging drivers to follow our advice by planning ahead, taking regular breaks and staying refreshed."

News.title: Serial killer 'worse than Jack the Ripper' raped, killed and mutilated 52 women
News.content:
Serial killer 'worse than Jack the Ripper' raped, killed and mutilated 52 women

WARNING, GRAPHIC CONTENT: Andrei Chikatilo was one of the world's most prolific serial killers, even eating some of his victims, who carried on murdering for more than two decades

View 4 Images Andrei Chikatilo was nothing short of a monster (Image: Sygma via Getty Images)

A notorious Russian serial killer raped and slaughtered at least 52 people, even consuming the body parts of some of his victims in one of the most disturbing cases of the last century.

‌



Andrei Romanovich Chikatilo, dubbed the Red Ripper among many other names, ranks among the most prolific serial killers ever. His horrifying crimes encompassed rape, murder, mutilation and cannibalism, spanning from 1978 until his capture in 1990.

‌



A police investigation into the sick killer was even hindered by the Soviet Union in the belief that serial killings were impossible in a Communist society. This mindset led to his initial arrest and subsequent release, which paved the way for further horrific murders.

‌



Following his second arrest, the Rostov Ripper confessed to the killing of 576 women and children. In 1992, he was tried for 53 murders and convicted for 52 of them.

View 4 Images Chikatilo said he killed 56 people- but was convicted of 52 murders (Image: Sygma via Getty Images)

Chikatilo was born in 1936 with hydrocephalus - or water on the brain - a condition that left him physically weak, plagued by humiliating bed-wetting well into his teens, and cursed with a lifelong struggle in the bedroom.

‌



His dad was a soldier captured during World War II, who returned home branded a “coward”.

In turn, Chikatilo became the perfect target at school - mocked, bullied and isolated. At just 15-years-old, the future killer's warped desires burst into reality as he allegedly attacked a young girl, climaxing instantly in a humiliating act that would define the rest of his life.

The ridicule that followed only deepened his twisted mind and forged a deadly link between sex and violence which would later claim dozens of innocent lives. In 1971, Chikatilo was employed as a teacher in the mining city of Novoshakhtinsk in Rostov.

‌



View 4 Images Police photos of Chikatilo wearing a suit and hat (Image: Time & Life Pictures/Getty Image)

In his various teaching positions, he brutally sexually assaulted children of both sexes. The first documented victim was nine-year-old Lena Zakotnova.

The depraved teacher lured her into an abandoned shed, where he attempted to rape her and, during the struggle, he reportedly slashed Lena with a knife and ejaculated simultaneously.

‌



Despite being seen with Lena before her disappearance, Chikatilo's wife provided him with an alibi, resulting in another person being arrested for the appalling crime. Still a free man, he carried on in his teaching role until mounting accusations of inappropriate conduct damaged his reputation.

By 1981, he lost his job in education and secured other employment as a factory clerk, which gave him access to several more potential victims. Chikatilo's second confirmed victim was 17-year-old Larisa Tkachenko, whom he strangled and stabbed to death.

Throughout this barbaric assault, the depraved murderer crammed soil and foliage into her mouth to silence her cries. Following Larisa's killing, Chikatilo established a horrifying method of targeting vulnerable young drifters and runaways.

‌



View 4 Images The mass murderer was executed in 1994 (Image: Sygma via Getty Images)

The gruesome nature of his offences sent shockwaves through the Soviet Union as it emerged he would entice defenceless youngsters into forests before savagely mutilating their private parts with a blade.

It is believed that he would remove his victims' intimate organs and eat them.

Article continues below

In some cases, he severed facial parts, including noses and tongues. His youngest victim was Igor Gudkov, just eight, who died in Aviators' Park in Rostov on August 9, 1983. While his eldest victim was Lyubov Zuyeva, 31, who died on April 4, 1990.

The dad-of-two received a death sentence for his atrocities and was executed by a single gunshot in the back of the head on February 14, 1994, aged 47. His remains were buried in an unmarked grave within the prison cemetery.

News.title: Girl, 4, killed as driver slammed into her and mum just feet from home
News.content:
A driver who killed a four-year-old girl in a crash as she walked home from Eid celebrations with her family has been jailed.

Javonnie Tavener, 23, previously pleaded guilty to causing death by careless driving after hitting two-year-old Mayar Yahia with his car on Upper Highgate Street in Birmingham on April 14, 2024. The crash also left two others - Mayar's mother, Sara, and another mother, Awadia Hammed - seriously injured. Tavener also admitted two counts of causing serious injury by careless driving and a further charge of causing death while driving uninsured.

At Birmingham Crown Court today, Tavener was jailed for three years and 10 months for offences related to careless driving. He was handed an additional 18-month sentence, to run consecutively, for assaulting and attempting to strangle his girlfriend several weeks later. He was also banned from driving for six years.

Image: BPM Media) BPM Media)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Two vehicles collided before a Corsa crashed through a barrier and into the group, the same court previously heard. Emergency services rushed to the scene and found Mayar on the roadside, where she was pronounced dead.

During the sentencing hearing, the judge was told that after the crash Tavener, of no fixed address, remained at the scene for nine seconds before reversing, causing Mayar to be dragged by the vehicle. He and a passenger then got out of the car and fled on foot, the court heard.

In a statement read to the court on Friday, Mayar's dad, Babiker Yahia, told how the family lived near the crash scene on Upper Highgate Street, giving them a "constant reminder" of the tragedy.

"I stayed with Mayar when she was taken from the collision and transported to hospital - that night will remain with me forever," he said.

He added that Mayar was the third of four children and was "full of love, always smiling" and "kind to everyone". "Mayar's siblings were with her at the time of the collision, [they] were young and witnessed it," he said. "They have been exposed to so much trauma, and we do not know how this will shape their lives as they grow."

Image: BPM Media) BPM Media)

Sentencing, His Honour Judge Peter Cooke said Tavener had driven at excessive speed while trying to overtake another vehicle in a 20mph zone at night, "with cannabis in your system and using a phone".

The judge continued: "The victim was a vulnerable pedestrian - you were on your phone, it was a car with no insurance. You said you were a rear passenger instead of the driver. The passenger's post incident conduct was a disgraceful as yours.

"You abandoned the car at the scene, after reversing, presumably to try to leave, dragging little Mayar. You walked off calling for your girlfriend.

"The callous way in which you walked away from the mayhem was sickening to watch. You blamed a phantom driver."

Detective Sergeant Paul Hughes, from the West Midlands Police serious collision investigation unit, previously said: "Mayar was just four years old when her life was tragically taken away from her. She was walking back with her family and friends after celebrating Eid.

"Pedestrians should be afforded and be reassured of protection when walking on the pavement and a split-second decision by Tavener to overtake where he did has led to this tragic chain of events. I would urge all drivers to think about this incident and how those decisions can have such catastrophic consequences."

News.title: Dubai Brits face three chilling threats if they film drone attacks
News.content:
Dubai Brits face three chilling threats if they film drone attacks

Brits have been issued with a chilling warning about the harsh penalties they could face for filming Iranian attacks currently blitzing the major holiday hotspot

View 4 Images A new warning has been issued to Brits in the UAE (Image: UGC/ANONYMOUS/AFP via Getty Imag)

The British embassy in the United Arab Emirates has revealed the grim fate Brits could face if they film Iranian missiles and drones flying over the holiday hotspot.

‌



Dubai, a state in the UAE, put out a new warning after a British man was arrested for allegedly filming strikes while on holiday, Detained in Dubai said. The holiday hotspot has been subjected to retaliatory strikes from Iran, after the US and Israel launched attacks which killed the supreme leader on February 28.

‌



The cities international airport, a hotel and luxury skyscrapers have all been hit by Iran. Despite the ongoing war in the Middle East, the UAE has appeared to crack down on people recording or posting about these attacks.

‌



View 4 Images A building in Dubai's financial district partially damaged from falling debris (Image: AP)

Dubai Brits who fled face having to go BACK despite Iran missile strikes READ MORE :

All the airlines hiking prices as jet fuel costs soar amid war in Middle East READ MORE :

On Friday the British Embassy in the UAE posted on X: "UAE authorities warn against photographing, posting, or sharing images of incident sites or projectile damage as well as government buildings and diplomatic missions."

It revealed the three punishments that Brits or someone of any nationality could face if they break this law. The embassy added: "British nationals are subject to UAE laws, violations may lead to fines, imprisonment, or deportation."

‌



View 4 Images Fire in a luxury building in Dubai on Thursday (Image: X)

In a post on Instagram, the embassy said the UAE authorities have issued "several warnings" regarding the photographing, publishing or sharing of images and videos "documenting incident sites or damage resulting from the fall of projectiles or shrapnel".

The post continued: "Under UAE law, 'sharing' can include posting on social media platforms as well as sending or forwarding content via messaging applications. UAE law also restricts the photographing of certain sites, including government buildings and diplomatic missions."

‌



This comes after legal group Detained in Dubai said a 60-year-old man from London was one of 21 people charged under cyber-crime laws in connection with videos and social media posts relating to the recent missile strikes. The group added that the punishment if found guilty could be up to two years in prison.

View 4 Images Fire at the Fairmont The Palm hotel on February 28 (Image: X)

Radha Stirling, the group's chief executive, said the man had claimed he deleted the video from his phone when asked and had no intention of doing anything wrong. Stirling said: "The charges sound extremely vague but serious on paper. In reality, the alleged conduct could be something as simple as sharing or commenting on a video that is already circulating online."

Article continues below

She added: "Under UAE cybercrime laws, the person who originally posts content can be charged, but so can anyone who reshapes, reposts or comments on it. One video can quickly lead to dozens of people facing criminal charges." The Foreign Office has confirmed it is in contact with local authorities after a British man was detained in the UAE.

Boxing.title: Jake Paul suffers embarrassing hygiene gaffe during Donald Trump rally appearance as major wardrobe error exposes long-held insecurity
Boxing.content:
YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump at a political rally.

Paul joined Trump on stage at Verst Logistics in Kentucky and was given the chance to address the crowd.

The 29-year-old went viral on social media, not for his comments about Trump and American manufacturing, but instead about his appearance.

Many pointed out vast sweat patches that were visible on Paul's beige suit as he spoke to the crowd.

The patches were particularly prominent under his armpits, which were exposed further when he raised his arms to the crowd.

Paul had previously spoken about how sweating had long been a major insecurity of his.

Jake Paul gets on stage at the Trump rally and has the nastiest pit stains I've ever seen



(it also appears the crowd doesn't know who he is) pic.twitter.com/3Yc071tZzf — Aaron Rupar (@atrupar) March 11, 2026

YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump

Paul and his brother Logan had promoted his deodorant brand ahead of his fight with Mike Tyson back in 2024

Paul made the revelation back in 2024 when he launched a deodorant brand W, which he and his brother Logan promoted before and after his bout with Mike Tyson.

'Sweating has been a longtime insecurity of mine,' Paul had said when launching the brand.

'If you know me, you know I'm a clean freak. I always want to smell fresh, but training in Puerto Rico's humid climate makes it a real challenge.

'I needed a product that could keep up with my lifestyle — one that smells amazing but isn't full of junk ingredients, and honestly, one that wasn't from 2006.

'That's why I started W… to create products for guys that will boost their confidence and smell amazing, but also don't cost $20 for a stick of deodorant.'

The W deodorants are claimed to offer 24 hours protection, with the brand website stating: 'With doubled scent load, increased hydration and more sweat-blocking power, our new deodorant and body wash are a monumental breakthrough for mankind (or at least for your morning)'.

During the event, Trump signalled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

Join the debate Did YOU spot Jake Paul's hygiene gaffe?

Trump had brought Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday in Kentucky

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

'And you have my complete and total endorsement.'

Paul had expressed his admiration for Trump credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul added. And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us.

'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'

The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavours in the boxing ring over recent years.

Boxing.title: Donald Trump gives Jake Paul shock endorsement for political career as boxer joins President at Kentucky rally
Boxing.content:
President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday and made a bold prediction about the social media star's future.

The 29-year-old Ohio native, who has transitioned from a YouTube personality to a major force in the combat sports world, stood alongside the president as the crowd in Hebron, Ky roared in support.

During the event, Trump signaled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

The president didn't stop at a mere prediction, immediately offering his political backing to the 'Problem Child' should he decide to pursue a seat in the future.

'And you have my complete and total endorsement,' the president added, cementing a public alliance with one of the most polarizing and popular figures in modern media.

President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience

Addressing the Kentucky crowd, Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

He told the crowd that the president had taught him the value of 'courage' and to 'never back down from a fight,' a mantra Paul has lived by throughout his boxing career.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision for the American Heartland.

'And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us,' he continued, signaling his belief in a resurgence of American manufacturing.

The fighter also leaned into a spiritual message, telling the audience: 'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.'

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless,' Paul concluded, before reflecting on the local spirit of the attendees in the Bluegrass State.

'I feel all the local Kentuckians feel the same way -- you guys have that fight, you guys have that swag. There's a lot of young kids in here. The future of America.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision

Paul's most recent endeavor in the boxing ring saw him take on British star Anthony Joshua

The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavors in the boxing ring over recent years.

Paul has transformed the boxing landscape by leveraging his massive digital following to create events that have outdrawn traditional championship bouts in viewership and revenue.

His most recent endeavor in the ring saw him take on British heavyweight star Anthony Joshua, which ended in a knockout victory for 'AJ'.



Whether Paul follows Trump's advice and swaps the gloves for a suit remains to be seen.

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone
Boxing.content:
Boxing superstar Anthony Joshua is quitting the UK to move to Dubai - even though the desert city is now in a warzone with thousands of Britons desperate to get out.

The former heavyweight world champion, 36, has formally confirmed he will now be resident in the United Arab Emirates despite it currently coming under nightly attack from Iranian suicide drones.

Joshua, who was born in Watford of Nigerian heritage, won a gold medal at the 2012 London Olympics and has since been awarded both OBE and MBE honours - but now is quitting his home country.

The surprise move by 'AJ' - as he is known to fans - is revealed in the small print of paperwork he filed on Friday for his £150million business empire in which he is legally required to state the country in which he lives.

And, extraordinarily, the documentation shows that Joshua has pressed ahead with his decision to change his residential status despite the war between the USA and Israel and Iran which has seen neighbouring UAE come under sustained attack.

Anthony Joshua is moving to Dubai - as revealed in the small print of his paperwork

Footage taken from social media shows Saturday's drone strike on Dubai International Airport

As well as the weather and facilities, Dubai is popular with the wealthy because there is no income tax there.

Joshua would have been liable to pay UK tax on his dividend payment of £10.1million in 2024 and £6.3million in 2023.

His company Sparta Promotions also made profits of £20.396million and paid £6.65million in UK tax in 2024.

Joshua confirmed the decision in the same week that recent UK sport emigre Rio Ferdinand and his wife Kate spoke about what it is like to live in Dubai while it is under missile attack.

Those attacks have seen thousands of Britons living in or visiting Dubai when the war broke out left desperate to get out - with some paying as much as £100,000 to secure evacuation on a private jet.

Filings for two of Joshua's companies, Sparta Promotions Limited and 258 Investments Limited, both show the formal change in where he will from now on reside.

Joshua has long been a regular visitor to Dubai where he uses the renowned sporting facilities for holidays and training, as well as promotional work for brands he represents.

The most spectacular stunt in this vein came in 2017 when he undertook a training sparring session on the helipad of the famous sail-shaped Burj Al Arab hotel - a 90-foot wide landing platform suspended 700 feet above the sea.

Passengers in the terminal at Dubai Airport on Saturday after the airport closed again due to drone strikes

He visited the city late last year, dining out with boxing promoter Eddie Hearn, and again last month when he had training sessions with MMA fighter Khabib Nurmagomedov.

The striking decision to shift base to Dubai follows the tragic road accident in Nigeria last year in which claimed the lives of two close friends, personal trainer Kevin 'Latz' Ayodele and long-time therapist Sina Ghami.

Joshua has been in recovery ever since the ordeal and recently posted pictures of rehab work on his ribs via Snapchat.

He captioned the post: 'Rehabilitation protocol. When you go thru certain things, you realise that you are stronger than you think you are.'

The move makes Joshua the latest high-profile wealthy Briton to be based overseas following the likes of Manchester United owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe and EasyJet founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou, who both live in Monaco, and Pimlico Plumbers tycoon Charlie Mullins, who is in Marbella.

The rules on tax for those with ex-pat status are complex.

If individuals spend 183 days or more in the UK they are considered a UK tax resident, but if it is fewer than 16 days they would not be considered resident and eligible for taxes.

For in-between cases, HMRC officials have a complex 'Statutory Residence Test' to determine whether UK tax has to be paid, weighing factors like how much time is spent in each place and how established their ties are in both countries.

Joshua is pictured emerging from the car crash that claimed his friends' lives

Joshua pictured with friends Latif Ayodele (centre) and Sina Ghami, who both died in the crash

So to maximise any tax saving Joshua could be forced to spend barely two weeks a year here.

In December it was revealed that 'AJ' had spent £1.2 million to secure a 15,300 sq ft penthouse, the Sky Palace, in the new Park Lane 101 development in Sultan Haitham City, in Oman, which is 300 miles away.

But it is thought the apartment is an investment property and it is not clear if he has yet settled on a permanent base in Dubai.

There has been speculation that the attacks on Dubai could see property prices there fall by as much as 20%.

Boxing.title: Amir Khan heads to Africa again as boxing promoters battle for control of the sport
Boxing.content:
Promoter Amir Khan is staging another boxing card in Africa, positioning himself in a rapidly shifting promotional landscape where global power brokers are increasingly battling for control of the sport.

Khan's latest event, set for Lagos, Nigeria, blends professional bouts with influencer boxing and regional rivalries - highlighting their strategy aimed at tapping into Africa's large youth audience and social media culture.

The card is topped by a crossover bout between Nigerian online personalities Carter Efe and Portable, who together boast around 10million followers and will settle a long-running online feud in the ring. It is being billed as one of the first major influencer boxing contests staged on the continent.

The event arrives at a time when boxing's promotional structure is undergoing significant upheaval. Frank Warren has been involved in a legal dispute connected to the Saudis, while rival promoter Eddie Hearn has also been navigating the growing influence of Dana White's new venture with TKO, Zuffa Boxing.

Against that backdrop, Khan has increasingly looked toward emerging markets rather than competing directly for the biggest heavyweight spectacles in the Middle East or Las Vegas.

His shows in Africa have mixed international fighters with local prospects and internet personalities, aiming to build audiences in regions where professional boxing infrastructure has historically been limited.

Amir Khan's vision for African boxing is moving faster than most realise. Behind the scenes, AK Promotions is already planning to stage four to five shows per year across the continent

October 2025 saw DAZN-broadcast 'Chaos in the Ring' card in Lagos, co-promoted with Dr. Ezekiel Adamu's Balmoral Group Promotions

The likes of Moses Itauma, David Adeleye and Lawrence Okolie all watched the event live

On the sporting side, the Lagos card features several all-African match-ups. Nigerian cruiserweight puncher Ezra Arenyeka (15-2, 12 KOs), known as the 'Nigerian King,' faces compatriot Godday Appah (14-2, 13 KOs) in a bout between two heavy-handed fighters from rival neighbourhoods in southern Nigeria.
